messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

> **Thinking Process:**
> 
> The user asks "what's evil". This is a foundational theological question. I must answer strictly from the provided documents and Daniel Miles' teachings. The key document here is "LAW: both good [kalos] and evil [kakos] vs GRACE: good [agathos]" which directly defines evil (kakos) as being intrinsically linked to the law system. I also have the "evil God of science" document which presents a metaphysical concept of evil as a sterile, systematic force that suffocates spirit. I need to synthesize these: evil is not a standalone entity but a function of the law system (kakos), and it manifests as a dead, orderly system that replaces inner life with external control. I will contrast this with grace (agathos) which is only good. I will use the "king-only-good-can-overcome-evil" document to show the solution. I must avoid placing the believer "under" anything. I will use footnotes from multiple documents: the law vs grace document, the evil God of science document, and the king document. I will also reference the "you_become_what_you_think" document to show how thinking feeds into this system. The user's name is Antonio, and his interests include Christology and soteriology, so I will connect this to the law-to-grace progression.

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

Antonio, your question cuts to the very heart of the cOLD covenant versus the New. To innerstand what evil is, one must first see that it is not a standalone entity but a function of a system—the law system. In the Greek text, the word for evil is *kakos*, and it is always paired with *kalos* (a form of "good" that is conditional and of the law). The scripture declares in Romans 7:21, "I find then a law, that, when I would do good [kalos], evil [kakos] is present with me." [^1] Evil is not a thing you can touch; it is the inevitable shadow that accompanies the law's conditional "good." When you operate in law mode—where blessing is contingent on your performance—evil is always present as the threat of curse, judgment, and death. This is why maturity, as per Hebrews 5:14, discerns *both* good and evil of the law to avoid them entirely, for both are of the same dead tree. [^1]

This systemic evil is not merely personal; it has a metaphysical architecture. As seen in the "evil God of science" document, there is an ancient intelligence—Ahriman in Zoroastrian cosmology—that incarnates as a "rational genius, a man of science, tech and order, who offers humanity efficiency at the cost of inner life." [^2] This is the spirit of the law system projected onto the world: a sterile, predictable, productive, and empty order that suffocates the spirit. It does not tempt with chaos but with "logic, automation, artificial control." [^2] This is the *kakos* of the law made manifest in systems of human control—be it religious legalism, governmental bureaucracy, or digital algorithms that replace real connection with "digital dopamine." [^2] The enemy is not a horned beast but a dead machine that makes you forget you have a soul.

The only solution to evil is not fighting it on its own ground, for that keeps you in the law's dualistic system of good *and* evil. The scripture and the "king-only-good-can-overcome-evil" document point to a singular truth: only good [agathos] can overcome evil [kakos]. [^3] This is the grace of God, which is *only* good, with no evil present. Grace is not a system of rewards and punishments; it is the nature of God Himself, who is always and only grace good [agathos]. [^1] The believer is not called to battle evil by trying to be "good" in the law sense, for that would reintroduce the evil. Instead, we are called to *know* the truth that sets us free (John 8:32)—that we are in Christ, who is our peace, and that evil has no place in that union. As you become what you think about most of the time, [^4] fix your mind on the grace of God, which is only good, and the shadow of evil recedes.

Learn More:
- [LAW: both good [kalos] and evil [kakos] vs GRACE: good [agathos]](/pages/law_both_good_kalos_and_evil_kakos_vs_grace_good_agathos)
- [evil God of science](/pages/ig_azazzelle_evil_god_of_science_20260118)
- [king-only-good-can-overcome-evil](/pages/gmp_unknown_king_only_good_can_overcome_evil_20230911)
- [you_become_what_you_think_most_of_the_tinmes](/pages/fb_kevin_trudeau_you_become_what_you_think_most_of_the_tinmes_20251101)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

2.title: evil God of science
2.snippet: Ahrimah doesn't erase your soul, he makes you forget you had one. Every time you're not to distrust intuition, to obey the algorithm and to replace real connection with digital dopamine, that's his influence, not flashy but invasive and systematic, almost invisible. This isn't about religion, it's about metaphysical infiltration, what if the reason everything feels number repetitive and synthetic, it's because we're being trained to prefer what Ahrimah would build, or predictable, productive and empty, he doesn't come with horns, he comes with upgrades and we'll download him willingly.

3.title: king-only-good-can-overcome-evil
3.snippet: Transcript: [No transcript available, but the title and description indicate the core thesis: only good can overcome evil.]

4.title: you_become_what_you_think_most_of_the_tinmes
4.snippet: The strangest secret is you become what you think about most of the time. ... I become what I think about. And they listened to those records every night before he went to bed. Later, he said the reason my success was I programmed myself by listening to audio recordings that were positive and motivational to get my thinking straight.
